clusters.canonicalTopic: AI engineering as a software discipline
clusters.synthesis: Broad framing of AI engineering as the evolving role of engineers in an AI-native software era.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Reasoning scaling and post-plateau model progress
clusters.synthesis: Focuses on reasoning training and scaling laws as the mechanism for continued model progress after an apparent plateau.
Treats maintainability as the ability to evolve codebases without creating regressions or future change friction.
Describes the loop where an agent acts in the world rather than only producing text.
All three candidates describe isolated, reproducible, or fleet-managed sandbox environments for safe agent execution.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Agent security and supply chain risk
clusters.synthesis: Covers attack surface expansion, short-lived credentials, malicious package-name abuse, access control, and ontology or supply-chain guardrails for agents.
Writing memory during execution through simple writable storage primitives.
A cluster around inner-loop evals, verifier separation, reward hacking, reliability, task-specific evaluations, regression gating, and using feedback to improve evaluation quality.
Both candidates focus on grounding AI behavior in organization-specific facts, capabilities, personas, and related reference material.
Working directly inside a customer codebase rather than as a detached service layer.
Both candidates describe reusable agent harnesses or packs that initiate work, bind tools and policies, and evolve into software-factory style execution units.
Using open-weight models as a practical production alternative.
Positions resumable execution as the stable core of an agent system.
Uses a generated site as a living representation of a person's thoughts, tastes, and work.

clusters.canonicalTopic: Agentic web perception and shared screens
clusters.synthesis: Centers on agents and humans looking at the same interface or screen to improve collaboration and reduce explanation.
Skills are treated as temporary capability boosts for tasks the model cannot yet do consistently.
Frames systems that investigate and repair themselves in repeated improvement loops.
These candidates all emphasize shared semantic structure, metadata projection, ontology rules, or graph modeling as substrate for enterprise context and knowledge organization.
All four describe durable, queryable context structures that preserve traces, moments, relationships, or work state for future use.
Focuses on repeatable browser rendering as a substrate for agent-facing web behavior.
Both candidates are about making model serving practical through specialization and local optimization rather than general-purpose scaling.
Argues that cheaper software creation expands the set of problems people want built.
Contrasts breadth and depth as a lens on modern product strategy.
The system is designed to preserve merchant identity and user trust while improving photos.
The idea that model usefulness can jump when paired with tools instead of only scaling parameters or context.
A metric-style way to measure whether a PR's ideas spread through the community and get reused.
